paper.title_en: Conductor plates in free-ghost infinite derivative electrodynamics
paper.abstract_en: We investigate the physical implications of a ghost-free infinite derivative Abelian gauge theory formulated via the Stueckelberg mechanism, which incorporates a non-local mass scale m, an infrared mass M, and a Stueckelberg mass mS. After deriving the covariant ghost-free propagator, we explore its connection with the Podolsky propagator in the local limit (). We obtain a closed-form expression for the interaction energy between static point charges, exhibiting a smooth regularization at short distances through a Gaussian error function. The analysis is extended to systems involving a single conducting plate, both perfect (μ → 0) and semi-transparent, where we derive modifications to the propagator and the resulting charge-plate interaction energy. Our results demonstrate the mechanism by which image method breakdown occurs in non-local electrodynamics and recover known results in the appropriate limits, providing a deeper understanding of boundary-induced effects in higher-derivative gauge theories.
